hero.title: Human-Centered Access. High-Compliance Infrastructure.
hero.subtitle: We believe the foundation of public benefits access is built at the business rules layer. Applied Policy Systems provides Independent Verification and Validation oversight and architects low-friction, highly regulated digital infrastructure, ensuring systems are a verifiable reflection of written law while reducing administrative burden.

challenge.label: Statutory Precision
challenge.title: The Translation Layer.
challenge.text: Public technology fails when system architecture diverges from legislation, exposing agencies to compliance risks and fiscal sanctions. We mitigate this risk by providing Independent Verification and Validation oversight and translating complex regulations into deterministic system logic. This ensures exact policy intent dictates operational behavior, protecting agency budgets from audit findings while reducing the administrative burden placed on residents and frontline caseworkers.
challenge.solution: We ensure system logic is a verifiable reflection of statutory requirements.
challenge.outputsLead: Standard verification artifacts and specialized operational deliverables include:

hero.label: About

founder.title: Graham O'Neill, Founder & Principal Consultant
founder.intro: Graham O'Neill is a systems architect and policy implementation expert dedicated to enforcing statutory compliance and mitigating fiscal risk in complex government systems.
founder.p1: Su carrera se centra en la integración técnica entre el acceso a beneficios centrado en el humano y la infraestructura de TI altamente regulada. From municipal revenue operations to federal advisory roles, Graham translates strict legislative mandates into functional operational realities.
founder.p2: Before founding Applied Policy Systems LLC, Graham held senior executive positions across all levels of government, specializing in the structural evaluation of benefits access, the oversight of eligibility infrastructure, and the implementation of secure tax administration programs.

m1.title: Systems Architecture
m1.desc: Architects neuro-symbolic rules engine prototypes combining Large Language Models and deterministic logic engines to automate public benefits eligibility calculations and evidence triage, ensuring mathematical accuracy for human adjudicators.

m2.title: State Executive Leadership
m2.desc: Executed the Predictive Benefits Access Initiative, integrating state IT infrastructure with plain-language mandates to prevent procedural drop-offs and protect resident due process.

m3.title: Systems Prototyping
m3.desc: Collaborated externally with civic tech prototype engineers to architect data-sharing agreements and integration logic, ensuring strict alignment with state IT security parameters.

m4.title: Policy Translation
m4.desc: Operated as a subject matter expert integrating public benefits and tax administration. Directed national policy initiatives and evaluated IRS quality control frameworks.

m5.title: Frontline Verification
m5.desc: Conducted direct client application assistance and utilized this engagement to author standard operating procedures and facilitate systemic quality control across multiple state jurisdictions.

m6.title: Federal Advisory
m6.desc: Serves in a concurrent advisory capacity to Congress and the IRS on electronic tax administration, providing subject matter expertise on federal privacy frameworks and data compliance.

hero.label: Operational Analytics
hero.title: Empirical Analysis & Systems Architecture.
hero.subtitle: This analytics layer serves as the foundational data source for our advisory engagements. We leverage decades of federal oversight data to engineer high-compliance frameworks.

adv1.title: Navigating the PL 119-21 Financial Penalties with Deterministic Rules-as-Code
adv1.desc: This document outlines the fiscal liabilities of Public Law 119-21, detailing how legacy rules engines and manual transcription workflows create artificially high Payment Error Rates. It provides a high-level architectural framework for implementing deterministic rules-as-code and a "Verify Once" strategy utilizing real-time electronic payroll data (e.g., The Work Number) to map exact federal policy intent to state system behavior, demonstrating the technological shift required to mitigate severe financial penalties.

adv2.title: Preventing Wrongful Denials and Title VI Audits Caused by Strict Verification
adv2.desc: This document establishes the correlation between rigid physical verification mandates and procedural error spikes. It details how the incentive for "Defensive Eligibility" directly triggers Title VI audit liabilities and wrongful denials. It presents a tiered, risk-based verification framework designed to exhaust electronic sources before requesting documentation ("Silent Verification") as the required operational correction.

adv3.title: Resolving Application Bottlenecks via Deterministic Queue Separation and Pre-Calculation
adv3.desc: This document diagnoses the structural failure of manual case processing in meeting 30-day federal processing mandates. It outlines how to implement deterministic queue separation and Intelligent Document Processing to automatically classify, understand, and extract key information from submitted work documents. By presenting merit-system caseworkers with a mathematically proven data schema, agencies can reduce processing time from weeks to seconds while strictly maintaining federal compliance regarding human eligibility determinations.

adv4.title: Satisfying IRS Pub 1075 Data Isolation Requirements for Ex Parte Renewals
adv4.desc: This document addresses the statutory conflict between CMS ex parte renewal mandates and the provisions of IRC § 7216 regarding the unauthorized disclosure of federal tax information. It defines the structural security overlays, strict logical data isolation requirements, role-based access controls, and Universal Consent Directives necessary to utilize federal tax data for eligibility matching without exposing the primary state mainframe to compliance liabilities.

adv5.title: Using Schema Validation to Bridge Modern Frontends with Legacy State Mainframes
adv5.desc: This document evaluates the integration failures of modern plain-language applications against MARS-E compliance standards and legacy system constraints. It identifies strict schema validation, API metadata constraints, and deterministic threshold enforcement as the mandatory data validation middleware required to safely ingest unstructured external data into legacy eligibility mainframes without exposing the state to audit liabilities.

adv6.title: Bypassing Monolithic Bottlenecks: Modular API Integration for Rapid Federal Mandates
adv6.desc: This document evaluates the structural inflexibility of monolithic builds in responding to sudden federal compliance mandates. It outlines the architectural necessity of independent, compliance-verified integration nodes that allow state agencies to rapidly deploy niche eligibility logic and APIs without breaking core legacy systems, triggering full-system regression tests, or incurring excessive vendor change-order bottlenecks.

documentTool.label: Prototype 01
documentTool.title: Document Intake & Verification
documentTool.desc: A dual-facing document evaluation tool. It gives applicants a clear, instant check to fix mistakes before submission and provides certainty when an upload meets program rules. For the agency, the system outputs diagnostic feedback and a mathematically provable audit trail to support the caseworker's final eligibility decision.

policyManual.label: Prototype 02
policyManual.title: Living Policy Manual
policyManual.desc: A conversational interface built for frontline caseworkers. It provides authoritative, legally grounded answers to complex eligibility questions, equipping caseworkers with a deterministic standard for case processing.

extractionEngine.label: Prototype 03
extractionEngine.title: Enterprise Rules Extraction Engine
extractionEngine.desc: A deterministic extraction tool for IT systems architects. It extracts executable logic gates and system parameters directly from dense legislative text for integration into state mainframes.
